results.title: Adjournment of Debate
results.summary: The debate was adjourned to continue tomorrow. No specific policy measures, legislative changes, or operational constraints were introduced or clarified during this session. The motion to conclude proceedings was moved by Mr Sharael Taha and accepted by the Speaker. No further details regarding the agenda for the resumed debate were provided in the transcript.

results.title: Active CPF Members Aged 55 from 2020 to 2024 and Future Projected Changes
results.summary: Active CPF members are defined as those with contributions paid in the current or preceding three months. Data from 2020 to 2024 shows the proportion of active members turning 55 experienced a slight uptrend despite pandemic-related labour market disruptions. The Ministry expects this proportion to remain stable in coming years, indicating no anticipated decrease in the active workforce entering age 55. Specific numerical counts for each year were referenced in the official response but are not detailed in this summary.

results.title: Addressing Deteriorating MRT Reliability and Measures to Address Root Causes
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport directed that the question regarding MRT reliability declines, breakdown causes, signalling effectiveness, and root cause measures has already been addressed in the oral reply to Parliamentary Questions 19 to 24 at the 22 September 2025 Parliament sitting. No new data, statistics, or specific policy actions were provided during this session. The response refers users to the official parliamentary record for the detailed answers to the four sub-questions raised by Mr Yip Hon Weng.

results.title: Adequacy of AML Controls for Financial Institutions Penalised for Breaching AML Requirements in 2016/2017 and 2025
results.summary: On 4 July 2025, the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) penalised nine financial institutions for breaching anti-money laundering obligations. While earlier breaches in 2016/2017 stemmed from system deficiencies in transaction monitoring, 2025 violations involved other areas such as customer risk assessment, source of wealth corroboration, and post-report follow-up. MAS noted that transaction monitoring systems were generally in place, with recent issues arising from inconsistent employee implementation rather than systemic failures. Affected institutions have accepted their shortcomings and implemented remediation measures, including stronger staff oversight. The Minister highlighted that criminals continuously shift tactics, necessitating that financial institutions adapt their control measures in tandem to maintain adequacy.

results.title: Allocation of Counsellors to Schools
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the Ministry of Education (MOE) deploys one to two trained counsellors per school based on specific needs rather than a single staffing ratio, citing approximately 220 counsellors in primary schools and another 220 in secondary schools. MOE conducts annual reviews of counsellor deployment using reported caseload and case complexity, providing additional resources like second counsellors or flexi-adjunct counsellors to schools with greater needs. The Government integrates mental health education into the curriculum from primary to pre-university levels and operates a Tiered Care Model to strengthen referral pathways between schools, community services, and hospitals for mild to moderate cases. For external support, students can access the REACH teams, national mindline 1771, CREST-Youth, and CHAT @ *SCAPE, while the Ministry of Health examines factors like parental consent to improve youth access to services.

results.title: Additional Measures to Tackle Increase in Cases of Government Officials Impersonation and Phishing Scams
results.summary: Government officials impersonation scams and phishing cases accounted for 28% of all scam instances and 34% of losses in the first half of 2025. To counter these, the Ministry will extend the "gov.sg" sender ID protocol to outgoing calls, ensuring agencies like the Singapore Police Force display a single common caller ID. Under the Online Criminal Harms Act, Meta will be directed to remove scam advertisements and profiles impersonating government officials, while other platforms must implement upstream verification measures. The Monetary Authority of Singapore is phasing out SMS One-Time Passwords for digital token users and rolling out FIDO-compliant hardware tokens to secure high-value transfers. Additionally, retail banks are adding verification steps for mobile wallet card provisioning. The public is advised to verify website authenticity, never disclose login details over calls, and consult the 24/7 ScamShield Helpline at 1799 if uncertain.

results.title: Alternative Arrangements of Public Library Resources for Sims Place’s Residents
results.summary: Under the National Library Board's 15-year Libraries of the Future Masterplan (2017–2030), the Geylang East Public Library will relocate to the Tanjong Katong Complex by 2030, positioning it near Paya Lebar MRT station. While the new site offers improved transport connectivity, NLB acknowledges the displacement for current Sims Place residents and is actively engaging the community to identify a nearby alternative space, such as a reading corner or pop-up library. Concurrently, the Board will collaborate with grassroots organisations to facilitate access to digital resources and delivery services for affected residents. This transition aims to balance proximity improvements with continued accessibility for all users, though specific timelines for the interim alternative space remain under identification.

results.title: Amount Recovered from Scams in 1H2025
results.summary: In the first half of 2025, the Police recovered over $56.7 million in scam losses, representing frozen funds in bank accounts and cryptocurrency wallets. Out of 19,665 reported cases, recovery efforts succeeded in approximately 7,200 cases, accounting for about 37% of the total. The Minister noted that recovery rates vary by case circumstances and remain challenging due to the rapid laundering of proceeds through complex payment networks and overseas transfers. Consequently, the full $456 million loss reported for the period has not yet been recovered.

results.title: Average and Median Attrition Rates of Teachers across 30-year Period
results.summary: For the periods 1995 to 2009 and 2010 to 2024, the mean and median annual teacher resignation rates in Singapore have consistently ranged between 2% and 3%. The Minister for Education stated that the 2025 resignation rate is expected to remain within this same range. No specific data was provided for the full 2010 to 2025 period, as the projection for 2025 was given separately from the historical statistics.

results.title: Average and Median Tenure Duration of Teachers across 30-year Period
results.summary: Minister for Education Desmond Lee stated that the median length of service for teachers was five to nine years from 1995 to 2009. For the period from 2010 to 2025, the median tenure duration increased to 10 to 14 years. The Minister did not provide data on the average tenure duration for either period, nor did he specify the exact years within the 2010 to 2025 range where the 10 to 14-year median applies. These figures reflect the median length of service rather than the average tenure duration requested.

results.title: Awareness, Utilisation Rates and Efficacy of Differentiated Job Matching Platforms for Fresh Graduates and Young Professionals
results.summary: Fresh graduates and young PMETs (aged 30 and below with diplomas or degrees) had a 33% active utilisation rate on the MyCareersFuture (MCF) portal in 2024, with approximately 60% of these users securing new jobs within six months. While job seekers utilise multiple channels including social media and recruitment agencies, Workforce Singapore (WSG) plans to boost MCF visibility through digital marketing, media pitches, and physical events. The agency will also enhance portal functionalities, such as search filters, to highlight opportunities linked to Government-supported schemes like the upcoming GRaduate Industry Traineeship (GRIT) programme. These measures aim to increase awareness and adoption of the portal alongside other job search avenues.

results.title: Banning Electronic Products on Flights
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that the Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (CAAS) aligns its safety regulations with International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) guidelines regarding electronic devices on flights. These regulations address risks like electromagnetic interference and lithium battery fire hazards by restricting allowable battery capacities and dictating carriage methods. While Singapore carriers must adhere to CAAS rules, they retain discretion to implement stricter policies. The Ministry uses these international standards as the primary criteria for deciding bans, working with global aviation authorities to determine the scope of restrictions.

results.title: Better Understanding of Provisional and Actual Suicide Statistics for More Responsive Policies and Community Interventions
results.summary: The provisional 2023 suicide death count in the Report on Registration of Births and Deaths is lower than the finalised figure because it excludes unnatural death cases pending Coroner's Inquiry outcomes, which are initially classified under "Symptoms, signs and abnormal clinical and laboratory findings, not elsewhere classified." These pending cases are subsequently reclassified as suicides after inquiries conclude, causing the annual statistic to be updated in the following year's report. Consequently, the finalised number for any given year will generally exceed the provisional number. The Immigration & Checkpoints Authority will provide an explanatory note in future reports to clarify this statistical disparity and aid responsive policy interventions.

results.title: Breakdown of Data on Residential Fires in HDB Flats by Cause and Type of Active Mobility Devices
results.summary: In the first half of 2025, fires involving Active Mobility Devices (AMDs) or their batteries in HDB flats accounted for less than 2%, totaling eight cases. Specifically, one fire involved Power Assisted Bicycles while seven involved Personal Mobility Devices. The Minister did not provide a breakdown for e-scooters, nor did he specify the exact proportion for the full year. No further details on fire causes or device types were stated for the remaining period.

results.title: Cause of 26 July 2025 Sinkhole and Measures to Prevent or Minimise Such Incidences
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that the question regarding the cause of the 26 July 2025 sinkhole on Tanjong Katong Road and future preventive measures has already been addressed via a written answer on 22 September 2025. Users are directed to consult the response recorded in the 22 September 2025 Order Paper for specific details on the incident's cause and the additional actions planned to minimise sinkhole occurrences on all roads. No new measures or updated timelines were announced during this session.

results.title: Certification Requirement for Power Banks Sold in Singapore
results.summary: Power banks sold in Singapore must comply with international safety standards specified in the Consumer Protection (Consumer Goods Safety Requirements) Regulations. The Consumer Product Safety Office (CPSO) under the Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore conducts regular market surveillance and enforces strict measures against non-compliance, which may result in fines or imprisonment. The Government will continue to monitor the situation closely and assess the need for additional pre-market requirements on the sale of power banks in Singapore. Consumers can find the latest information on product safety matters on the CPSO's website.

results.title: Challenges in Safeguarding Youths from Extremist Ideologies vis-a-vis Evolving Digital Landscape
results.summary: Since 2015, the Internal Security Department (ISD) has issued Internal Security Act orders against 18 youths aged 20 or below, nine of whom intended to mount attacks, all self-radicalised online. The threat landscape has diversified to include far-right and mixed ideologies, with radicalisation accelerating from weeks to months via digital echo chambers on social media, encrypted platforms, and gaming communities. To counter this, the Government strengthened legislative levers through the Online Safety (Miscellaneous Amendments) Act and Online Criminal Harms Act to block harmful content. A multi-pronged approach involves sensitising youths and educators via ISD-MOE workshops, joint online guides for parents, and curriculum integration in Social Studies and Character and Citizenship Education. Community partners like the Religious Rehabilitation Group conduct outreach through counter-ideology forums, while public education initiatives such as the National Library Board's S.U.R.E. Programme and MHA's 2024 SGSecure social media accounts aim to build resilience against misinformation and radicalisation.

results.title: Common Safety Lapses Identified Through Companies that Participated in June 2025's Voluntary Safety Timeout
results.summary: Following five workplace fatalities in May 2025, the Ministry of Manpower (MOM) urged companies to conduct voluntary Safety Time-Outs from 12 to 26 June 2025, focusing on vehicular and work-at-height activities. MOM did not track participation numbers but conducted over 500 inspections in high-risk sectors between April and June 2025, issuing three stop-work orders and 110 fines. Common safety lapses identified included unguarded open sides, inadequate guardrails, and missing traffic management plans. The Ministry emphasized these specific hazards during the inspection period.

results.title: ComLink+ Take-up Rates among Eligible Families
results.summary: ComLink+ currently supports approximately 10,000 eligible families, while an additional 4,000 eligible families have declined participation. The Minister for Social and Family Development confirmed these figures during the debate on take-up rates. No specific timeline for future recruitment or reasons for the decline were provided in the transcript. The programme remains open to eligible families who have not yet opted out.

results.title: Complaints against Employee Monitoring beyond Working Hours, and Enforcement Actions and Employee Recourse Available
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower does not track complaints regarding intrusive employee monitoring outside working hours as these practices fall outside the scope of the Employment Act. Consequently, no statistics on complaint volumes, enforcement action percentages, or specific recourse measures are available from the Ministry. Employees are directed to first address privacy concerns through their company's internal grievance channels or by approaching their union if applicable. For further assistance, employees may contact the Tripartite Alliance for Fair and Progressive Employment Practices.

results.title: Companies Failing to File Mandatory Retrenchment Notifications and Incurring Penalties across Five Years
results.summary: From January 2021 to June 2025, the Ministry of Manpower received approximately 19,800 Mandatory Retrenchment Notification (MRN) submissions, with 5,750 filed late beyond the five-working-day requirement. Most non-compliant employers received caution letters and subsequently cooperated to file their notifications. The Minister did not disclose the specific number of companies that incurred penalties for failing to comply with this requirement during the stated period.

results.title: Completion Rates of Lasting Power of Attorney across Sociodemographic Profiles and Possible Extension beyond 31 March 2026
results.summary: As of 15 August 2025, the Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF) has registered over 350,000 Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA) documents from Singaporeans. Demographic data indicates that 93% of donors are Chinese, 63% are aged 50 to 75, and 58% are female; income data is not collected. While MSF does not currently collect income information, it encourages all citizens to complete their LPA early for family clarity. Regarding the filing fee waiver, MSF will consider extending the current waiver beyond 31 March 2026 but has not yet confirmed a decision or provided a specific timeline for this extension.

results.title: Considerations When Adding Bus Routes and Increasing Frequency Bus Services in A Precinct
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority evaluates ridership demand, journey times, travel patterns, alternative options, and resource availability when adding bus routes or increasing frequency in specific precincts. For new Housing and Development Board estates, the agency assesses the feasibility of pre-emptive bus service deployment. This decision-making process is strictly constrained by the limited pool of available bus drivers. Consequently, service expansions are not automatic but depend on balancing operational capacity against identified transport needs and existing infrastructure limitations.

results.title: Considerations for Singapore’s Position on Cross-Border Ride-Hailing
results.summary: Since July 2025, the Land Transport Authority (LTA) has intensified enforcement against unlicensed foreign vehicles providing point-to-point services within Singapore, having caught over 70 vehicles to safeguard commuter safety and protect licensed local drivers. Illegal services lack valid insurance and employ unlicensed drivers, posing serious accident risks. The Government will continue enforcing against illegal rides by increasing penalties and pushing for the removal of facilitating apps and advertisements. Simultaneously, the Ministry is discussing with Malaysian authorities to enhance the Cross-Border Taxi Scheme and develop new options for large families, the elderly, and corporate customers. ComfortDelGro and Strides Premier will accept phone and online bookings for doorstep pick-ups under the existing scheme to provide more earning opportunities. The Government is also exploring location tracking, including electronic road pricing 2.0, to prevent illegal trips by cross-border taxis while balancing user convenience with driver livelihood safeguards.

results.title: Considerations of Mixed-use Developments Managed Under Single MCST
results.summary: The majority of strata mixed-use developments in Singapore, including Compass One and Compass Heights, operate under a single-tier Management Corporation Strata Title (MCST) structure. The Minister for National Development confirmed that these specific projects utilize this single-tier arrangement rather than a multi-tier system. No further details regarding the specific considerations for allowing this structure were provided in the response. Consequently, the transcript does not elaborate on the broader policy rationale or list other developments currently managed under a single MCST.

results.title: Consolidating and Coordinating Suicide Data to Formulate Targeted Support
results.summary: The Minister stated Singapore adopts a whole-of-society strategy to address suicides, utilizing public education, trained peer supporters, and the national mindline 1771 service for 24/7 distress support. The Interagency Taskforce on Mental Health and Well-being launched the National Mental Health and Well-being Strategy in October 2023, which supports all population segments through a Tiered Care Model tailored to mental health severity. The government will continue reviewing measure effectiveness to ensure targeted protective support for those at risk. No specific national suicide prevention strategy timeline was provided beyond the October 2023 strategy launch.

results.title: Correlation between Number of Trains Operating on NEL during Peak and Capacity Utilisation
results.summary: SBS Transit currently operates up to 40 trains on the North-East Line (NEL) during peak hours (7.30 am–9.30 am), an increase from 36 trains in 2024, reducing wait times to under two minutes. While most commuters board the first arriving train, some may wait an additional two minutes for the second train. To alleviate congestion beyond train frequency, the Land Transport Authority (LTA) has enhanced five existing and added five new peak-hour bus services for the Northeast Region. Additionally, LTA improved incentives for the Travel Smart Journeys programme to encourage commuters to shift their travel timing or mode. Regarding future capacity, the Cross Island Line (CRL) Phase 1 and Punggol Extension are on track for completion by the early 2030s, and feasibility studies are underway for a new Seletar Line to provide alternative routes from the northeast to the city centre.

results.title: Criteria to Revoke Permanent Residency or Work Pass when Committing Criminal Offences in Singapore
results.summary: Foreigners, including Permanent Residents and Work Pass holders, may face pass cancellation and re-entry bans if they commit criminal offences or engage in undesirable conduct. The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs stated that decisions rely on a framework assessing the incident's facts, nature, and severity, alongside the individual's family roots and contributions to Singapore. Specific circumstances for revocation are not explicitly defined in the transcript, as the assessment remains case-by-case based on these criteria.

results.title: Data on Eligible Children with No or Inactive CDA Accounts and Barriers to Open and Utilise Such Accounts
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development stated that eligible Singapore Citizen children can open a Child Development Account (CDA) anytime from birth until the end of the year they turn 12. Across the 2013 to 2024 birth cohorts, approximately 420,000 children are eligible, with less than 1% failing to open an account. The Minister noted that a primary barrier preventing account opening is for parents residing overseas who are unable to utilise CDA funds outside Singapore. No other specific barriers were detailed in the response.

results.title: Data on Foreign Online Influence Operations Detected
results.summary: The Government does not disclose specific statistics on foreign online influence operations detected between 2022 and 2025 due to operational reasons. Instead, it cites July 2024 account restrictions against 95 social media accounts and October 2024 blocking of 10 inauthentic websites as examples. Identified techniques include coordinated social media amplification, masquerading foreign-origin sites as local entities using familiar visuals, and deploying artificial intelligence-generated content. Details on platforms, reach, and the number of takedowns or counter-messaging actions remain undisclosed.

results.title: Data on Individuals Assisted by SkillsFuture Jobseeker Support Scheme Since Launch
results.summary: As of 31 August 2025, Workforce Singapore received over 7,200 applications for the SkillsFuture Jobseeker Support Scheme launched in April 2025, with approximately 40% approved and 60% rejected. The scheme strictly targets involuntarily unemployed jobseekers aged 26–40 who have worked at least six months in the past year and have an average monthly income not exceeding $5,000. Eligibility is denied to freelancers or those with non-renewal contracts, as the programme excludes voluntary leavers and those whose unemployment reasons remain unverified. While the majority of rejections stem from failing these specific criteria, the Minister confirmed that scheme parameters will be reviewed regularly to ensure support reaches those facing involuntary unemployment due to retrenchment, business cessation, or dismissal.

results.title: Data on Jobseekers Finding Jobs After Being Retrenched for More than a Year and Possible "Returnship" Programme
results.summary: Of the 9,990 residents retrenched in 2023, 80.8% secured employment within 18 months. The Ministry of Manpower and Workforce Singapore offer existing schemes like the Mid-Career Pathways Programme for mature workers aged 40+ and Career Conversion Programmes for reskilling unemployed individuals for at least six months. Enhanced salary support is available for employers hiring CCP participants, while grants like the Senior Employment Credit and Part-Time Re-employment Grant assist in hiring seniors and offering flexible work. A Committee on Managing Impact of Restructuring is developing proactive support for displaced workers following the Economic Strategy Review, with details to follow. Additionally, a Tripartite Workgroup on Senior Employment will review policies next year, and employers can be identified via Tripartite Standards logomarks on MyCareersFuture for age-friendly practices.

results.title: Data on Retail Premises Subject to Code of Conduct for Leasing of Retail Premises
results.summary: The Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Trade and Industry stated that the requested breakdown of retail premises subject to the Code of Conduct for Leasing of Retail Premises is unavailable. This is because lease terms constitute private agreements between landlords and tenants, preventing the government from collecting or disclosing specific data on lease sizes or intervals. Consequently, no statistics can be provided for premises ranging from 1,000 to 5,000 square feet or those exceeding 5,000 square feet. The Minister did not offer alternative data sources or revised timelines for future disclosure.

results.title: Dementia and Osteoporosis Rates Among Seniors Over 70 and Preventive Screening Plans
results.summary: Based on 2023 data, approximately 11% of seniors over 70 have dementia and 16% have osteoporosis. The Ministry is developing care protocols under the Healthier SG Programme to address these conditions. These protocols will incorporate evidence-based screening assessments and clinical management strategies for pre-dementia and osteoporosis within the community. Specific timelines for programme inclusion or rollout were not stated in the response.

results.title: Disclosing Emissions and Carbon Tax Relief of Individual Companies and Their Facilities to Subject Them to Greater Scrutiny
results.summary: The Ministry plans to release aggregated data on carbon tax allowances in 2027, balancing transparency with commercial sensitivities. Allowances are not a free pass for emissions-intensive firms but are judiciously assigned for only a proportion of Scope 1 emissions. Recipient companies must submit credible net-zero decarbonisation plans that the Government reviews and holds them accountable for. The Ministry will adjust allowances based on delivery on decarbonisation plans, international developments, and advancements in decarbonisation technologies.

results.title: Deploying AI for Audit Checks and Monitoring of Government Agencies' Contract Management and Procurement
results.summary: The Auditor-General's Office (AGO) currently employs a risk-based audit approach utilizing digital tools, including artificial intelligence (AI), to analyse procurement trends, detect irregularities, and identify higher-risk transactions. AGO has developed specific AI tools to flag potentially fictitious documents and intends to share its risk indicators and detection techniques with public agencies. The Government is actively exploring AI capabilities to augment finance, procurement, and contract management work, with some agencies already using shared tools like AI-powered anomaly detection and document analysis for fraud detection. While AGO plans to continue trialling emerging AI tools to enhance audit capabilities, specific implementation timelines for cross-agency deployment were not stated in the response.

results.title: Employment Outcomes for Unemployed Individuals who Utilised SkillsFuture Credit, SkillsFuture Career Transition and SkillsFuture Level-Up Programmes
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the Ministry will provide an oral reply regarding employment outcomes for unemployed individuals who utilised SkillsFuture Credit, the SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme, and the SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme. This response will address which jobseeker segments experienced lower success rates and the Ministry's plans to narrow the gap between skills upgrading and actual job placements. The detailed data and strategic measures will be presented at the next available opportunity alongside other related Parliamentary Questions filed on this topic. No specific statistics, timelines, or eligibility constraints were disclosed in the current transcript.

results.title: Development of Frameworks to Track Recent Graduates' Long-term Employability and Mitigate Risks of Skills Obsolescence
results.summary: The Government monitors fresh graduate employment via the Ministry of Education's Graduate Employment Survey and Ministry of Manpower's Labour Force Survey, noting stable six-month employment rates and median salary increases of 3% to 5% in 2024. Institutes of Higher Learning align curricula with industry needs through regular reviews, partnerships for co-designed courses, and expanded interdisciplinary offerings. Graduation requirements now mandate internships or industry attachments for polytechnics, ITE, and most autonomous universities, while students gain hands-on AI experience through Centres of Innovation and Excellence. IHLs also integrate industry-relevant AI tools to build digital literacy and critical thinking skills, ensuring graduates possess competencies like collaboration and cross-cultural adaptability to navigate an AI-driven economy and mitigate skills obsolescence risks.

results.title: Encouraging More Schools to Let Students Who Are Not on School Teams Participate in Sports Tournaments
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated the Ministry encourages all students, including those not on official school teams, to participate meaningfully in sports through school-based inter-class games and external competitions organised by ActiveSG and National Sports Associations. In 2023, over 27,000 students engaged in these external events, with nearly half not part of official teams. The Ministry welcomes school-initiated programmes like the "Glory and Grit 7s" tournament and supports schools adopting other approaches, such as collaborating with sports organisations, to create additional competition opportunities tailored to their context for students passionate about sports.

results.title: Enforcement Actions to Address Illegal U-turns and Zebra Crossing Violations
results.summary: The Traffic Police will deploy new Traffic Violation Enforcement Cameras by 2026 to address illegal U-turns and other violations. While officers are deployed islandwide with a focus on accident-prone locations and hotspots, the specific number of personnel varies based on operational needs and manpower constraints. The Ministry does not focus enforcement solely on illegal U-turns and zebra crossing violations. Details regarding the latest average monthly deployment of enforcement personnel were not provided in the response.

results.title: Enhancing Vape Detection in Schools and Counselling for Students Caught Vaping
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) clarifies that schools and Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs) employ diverse detection strategies beyond vape detectors, utilizing protocols, nicotine test kits after parental notification, and student-led peer support networks. Effective 1 September 2025, baseline disciplinary measures including detention, suspension, caning for boys, and conduct grade adjustments are enforced in schools, while IHLs apply stricter actions such as hostel eviction, privilege withdrawal, community service, and fines. Crucially, students who voluntarily seek help to quit vaping will not face disciplinary measures, with rehabilitation supported by in-house counsellors and referrals to external services like the Health Promotion Board's QuitLine. The framework balances firm consequences with sustained support, family engagement, and coordinated follow-up to prevent recidivism, alongside embedded anti-vaping education in curricula and regular parent outreach via the Parents Gateway.

results.title: Enquiry on 2027 Target Goals for Development Support and Learning Support (DS-LS) Programme in Preschools
results.summary: As of end-2024, over 900 preschools offer the Development Support and Learning Support (DS-LS) programme, covering 68% of preschoolers aged five to six. The Early Childhood Development Agency has met its 2025 target of covering 60% of eligible children and is on track to reach the 2027 steady-state goal of covering 80% of preschoolers aged five to six. The DS-LS programme specifically serves children requiring low levels of Early Intervention support.

results.title: Establishing GenAI Guidelines across Autonomous Universities and Penalties against Misuse
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the Ministry will provide an oral reply regarding plans to issue regulations for autonomous universities on GenAI usage rules, disciplinary due process, and penalties. This response will address questions on distinguishing assistive tools from content generation, procedural requirements for misuse allegations, and proportional penalties across institutions. The specific details, timelines, and policy measures will be communicated at the next available opportunity alongside other related Parliamentary Questions. No immediate directives or finalized guidelines have been announced in this session.

results.title: Evaluating MediSave Withdrawal Limit to Address Healthcare Costs, Evolving Medical Technologies and Changing Needs
results.summary: MediSave withdrawal limits are reviewed regularly to balance current expenditure with future healthcare needs. This year, limits for the Flexi-MediSave Scheme and Outpatient Scans increased to $400 and $600 respectively. Additionally, Flexi-MediSave eligibility now covers costlier dental treatments such as root canals and permanent crowns. The Ministry considers enhancements carefully to ensure sufficient MediSave reserves remain for major acute episodes, particularly as citizens age. No specific timeline for future reviews or public consultations was stated in the response.

results.title: Extending CDC Vouchers Scheme beyond Merchants Located Near HDB Estates and Hawker Centres
results.summary: The Minister clarified that the CDC Vouchers Scheme, introduced in June 2020 to assist households and support hawkers, primarily targets merchants within HDB heartlands and National Environment Agency-managed centres. While the scheme has onboarded over 23,000 eligible hawkers and merchants to date, expansion beyond these areas occurs only on a case-by-case basis where few or no heartland merchants exist near residential estates. The Ministry continues to collaborate with partners like the Federation of Merchants' Association to identify and onboard eligible small businesses providing essential daily goods or services in such locations. No specific timeline or new eligibility criteria for extending the scheme to non-HDB proximate merchants were announced.

results.title: Extending Climate Vouchers to Tenants Under Single Room Shared Facilities to Encourage Take-up of Climate-friendly Appliances
results.summary: Under the enhanced Climate Friendly Households Programme, eligible Singapore Citizen and Permanent Resident HDB households can claim $400 vouchers for resource-efficient appliances. However, tenants in HDB Single Room Shared Facilities are excluded from purchasing their own appliances because units are furnished with energy and water-efficient fixtures, and shared facilities like kitchens and laundry rooms are provided. Consequently, the National Environment Agency does not currently extend vouchers to these tenants, as they do not need to purchase the specific appliances covered by the scheme. The agency will continue to periodically review the programme's coverage.

results.title: Extension of Tuition Fee Loans and CPF Education Loan Scheme to Working Adults who Take Up Part-time Degrees
results.summary: Working adults who are Singapore Citizens pursuing MOE-subsidised part-time undergraduate or postgraduate research degrees at autonomous universities can access Government tuition fee loans, their Post-secondary Education Account, or SkillsFuture credit. Government bursaries are also available to eligible part-time undergraduates from lower- and middle-income families. However, the CPF Education Loan Scheme remains restricted to local full-time tertiary courses at approved institutions to safeguard CPF savings for retirement, healthcare, and housing needs. This clarification confirms current eligibility boundaries for mid-career skills development financing.

results.title: Factors Triggering Increased Public Sector Hiring to Support Singaporeans and Fresh Graduate Vacancies in Public Service
results.summary: The Government monitors GDP growth, sectoral value-added, unemployment rates, and retrenchment data to determine if increased public sector hiring is triggered during economic downturns, rather than relying on specific thresholds. As of 11 September 2025, approximately 45% of the projected 2,400 fresh graduate vacancies have been filled, with an additional 300 new positions created since 10 July 2025. To enhance awareness, the SG Public Service Career Fair on 23 August 2025 attracted around 1,800 fresh graduates. Starting October 2025, the GRIT@Gov Traineeship Programme will be introduced to provide practical skills and experience to strengthen fresh graduate employability. The Government will continue to monitor economic conditions and labour market trends to address emerging concerns and evolving agency needs.

results.title: Feasibility Study on Regulating Chance-based Monetisation Mechanism in Video Games to Safeguard Young Consumers
results.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs and the Gambling Regulatory Authority will review suggestions to implement probability disclosure and parental consent measures for loot boxes in Singapore. This review aims to reduce gambling inducement risks associated with chance-based monetisation mechanics in video games, particularly safeguarding youths. The decision to adopt these specific regulatory measures, which mirror developments in the US, South Korea, and China, remains under consideration following the recent global context. No specific timeline for the review or implementation was stated in the transcript.

results.title: Findings of Past and Current Reviews on School Policies towards Bullying and Hurtful Behaviours
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the question regarding the last review of school bullying policies, its outcomes, the current review's completion date, and the public release of findings has already been addressed in the oral reply to Questions Nos 1 to 12 on 23 September 2025. No new details, timelines, or specific outcomes were provided in this session as the matter was previously covered. The full findings of the current review and their public availability remain as previously outlined in the referenced oral response.

results.title: Fines and Deterrents Imposed on Domestic Workers and Employers for Unpermitted Multi-household Services from 2020 to Date
results.summary: From 2020 to 2024, the Ministry of Manpower (MOM) imposed fines on illegal employers and migrant domestic workers (MDWs) providing unpermitted multi-household services, with average fines of $3,100 for employers and $3,000 for workers in administrative cases. For prosecuted cases, courts imposed average fines of $8,800 on employers and $5,000 on workers based on individual liability and circumstances. MOM issues warnings for short-duration, few-occasion violations rather than immediate fines. The Ministry considers factors including duration of illegal employment when calibrating enforcement actions to achieve punitive and deterrent objectives, rather than solely relying on income levels.

results.title: Helping Artistes Build Commercially Sustainable Careers while Safeguarding Artistic Integrity
results.summary: Under the SG Arts Plan (2023-2027), the National Arts Council (NAC) supports artistes through grants, capability development via the Arts Resource Hub, and a Skills Framework for the Arts partnership with SkillsFuture Singapore. Between 2022 and 2024, over 190 artists utilized the Marketing and Audience Development Grant to showcase works internationally. NAC facilitates market access through three major festivals, including SIFA 2025 which engaged 360+ participants and 77,000 audience members, alongside local initiatives like ArtsEverywhere@CDC and overseas representation at the Frankfurt Book Fair since 2023. To broaden audience reach, NAC launched the SG Culture Pass on 1 September to encourage Singaporeans to support the sector. The Minister emphasized that building a sustainable arts ecosystem requires a whole-of-society effort involving government, partners, and the public.

results.title: Impact of Indonesia's Recalibration of LNG Trade on Singapore's Energy Security
results.summary: Indonesia remains a primary natural gas supplier for Singapore, with current pipeline contracts expiring in end-2028; however, Indonesia's shift toward US LNG imports will not affect Singapore as the nation is not a gas exporter. To mitigate supply risks from growing domestic Indonesian demand, the government has diversified sources so that no single country supplied over 30% of imports in 2024, while LNG now constitutes nearly 60% of total natural gas supplies. Strategic measures include operating an LNG terminal since 2013, constructing a second terminal to be operational by the end of the decade, and installing at least two Gigawatt-peak of solar capacity by 2030. Additionally, plans aim to import approximately six Gigawatt of low-carbon electricity by 2035, representing one-third of projected energy demand, alongside feasibility studies for geothermal, low-carbon ammonia, and nuclear energy.

results.title: Impact of Revisions to Vehicular Emissions Scheme Rebates and EV Early Adoption Incentive on COE Prices
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority and National Environment Agency reviewed the Vehicular Emissions Scheme and Electric Vehicle Early Adoption Incentive based on EV and hybrid adoption rates and total cost of ownership. Consequently, rebates for Band A1 vehicles were lowered, VES rebates were excluded for hybrids, and the EEAI will cease after 2026. These adjustments aim to narrow the upfront cost gap between cleaner-energy and Internal Combustion Engine cars. Members are directed to the Acting Minister's response to the Adjournment Motion on 22 September 2025 for further context regarding these policy changes.

results.title: Impact of Rise of AI on Gender Employment Gap
results.summary: The Minister stated that the Government has yet to find evidence supporting the hypothesis that AI will disproportionately affect female employment in Singapore, noting that the gender employment rate gap narrowed from 18.8 percentage points in 2014 to 10.5 percentage points in 2024. To harness AI's potential while ensuring inclusive growth, the National AI Strategy 2.0 aims to expand the pool of AI practitioners and uplift workforce capabilities through initiatives like the Enterprise Workforce Transformation Package, which allocates over $400 million for job redesign, and the NTUC Company Training Committee Grant providing around $200 million for workforce enhancement. Support for workers includes Career Conversion Programmes, the TechSkills Accelerator, and the AI Apprenticeship Programme to transition into roles such as software and cloud professionals, alongside Workforce Singapore's HerCareer initiative and flexible work arrangements effective from 1 December last year to facilitate women's career progression.

results.title: Impact of Tokenize Xchange Operations Cessation and Recourse for Customers
results.summary: Following the Monetary Authority of Singapore's rejection of Amazingtech Pte Ltd's (ATPL) licence application, the unlicensed operator of Tokenize Xchange was ordered to wind down operations and was subsequently placed under interim judicial management on 15 August 2025. As ATPL was not regulated by MAS, the government does not hold specific data on the number of impacted customers or exact fund amounts, though interim judicial managers estimate total customer claims at $266.3 million as of 15 August 2025. By 9 September 2025, 2,241 customers had registered their details with the appointed managers, who advise affected parties to submit information if they have not already done so. The MAS maintains that crypto assets are unsuitable for retail investors and warns the public to deal only with licensed entities while avoiding high-risk schemes such as rug-pulling or pump-and-dump operations.

results.title: Implementation and Raising Awareness for New Motorised PMA Rules
results.summary: The Ministry of Transport will table legislative amendments in October 2025 to implement new Personal Mobility Aid (PMA) regulations in the first quarter of 2026. These measures reduce the speed limit from 10km/h to 6km/h, align PMA dimensions with public transport requirements, and mandate medical certification for mobility scooters to curb misuse by able-bodied users. To facilitate a smooth transition, the Land Transport Authority will launch publicity campaigns using infographics and education materials, while enforcement officers actively engage users to remind ineligible individuals to switch to alternative mobility devices. The timeline is designed to accommodate retailers, the healthcare industry, and device users.

results.title: Implementation of New Guidelines to Provide Clarity in Job Postings on Employment Platforms
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower will not mandate new guidelines requiring employers to lock in salary ranges, detailed job descriptions, or recruitment timelines on employment platforms, as such requirements could limit applicant pools and hinder the shift from a "plug and play" to a "plug, train and play" hiring approach. Similarly, the Ministry will not require employers to furnish unsuccessful interviewees with specific reasons for rejection, citing significant operational burdens, compliance costs, and the involvement of confidential factors in hiring decisions. While the Tripartite Guidelines on Fair Employment Practices already stipulate that selection criteria should be clearly stated, the Ministry encourages employers to provide details voluntarily. The Workplace Fairness Act, upon implementation, will safeguard merit-based hiring and provide recourse for jobseekers subjected to discrimination, rather than mandating individualized rejection feedback.

results.title: Improving Recycling Rates within Businesses and Residential Areas and Creating One-stop Recyclables Collection Centres
results.summary: The Ministry for Sustainability and the Environment reported ongoing efforts to boost domestic recycling rates through extended producer responsibility schemes, progressive food waste segregation for large commercial generators, and increased collection frequency under the National Recycling Programme. The beverage container return scheme for plastic and metal containers is scheduled to launch next April. While progress has been made in reducing waste generation, the Minister noted that improving the overall recycling rate remains a challenge due to contamination issues. The National Environment Agency continues public education via the Recycle Right campaign and supports community partners, including Public Waste Collectors and Town Councils, in trialing smart recycling bins and co-locating dedicated bins in malls and HDB void decks. Details on strengthening support for paper recycling will be shared when ready.

results.title: Increasing Financial Support for Seniors Living Alone for Healthcare, Social Services and Housing
results.summary: The Government has enhanced subsidies for seniors living alone, particularly lower-income individuals or those with limited family support, across healthcare, social support, and housing. Budget 2025 introduced additional long-term care subsidies for cohorts born in 1969 or earlier, while the Silver Support Scheme will increase quarterly cash payments to eligible seniors from $900 to $1,080 starting in 2025. Housing options include shorter-lease 2-room Flexi flats and Community Care Apartments, with the Silver Housing Bonus offering up to $40,000 for right-sizing from December 2025. Additional assistance comes through the permanent GST Voucher scheme, MediSave top-ups, and the EASE programme for home fittings. The Silver Generation Office conducts outreach to connect seniors aged 60+ with services, while Age Well Neighbourhoods pilot senior-friendly infrastructure in existing estates.

results.title: Introduction of Protected Time Regulations during Working Hours in Support of Breastfeeding Mothers
results.summary: The Government will not introduce regulations mandating protected pumping time for breastfeeding mothers, opting instead for an enabling approach that encourages employers to adopt supportive practices voluntarily. Current frameworks include the Tripartite Standard on Work-Life Harmony, which recommends lactation support and flexible breaks, alongside the Employers' Guide to Breastfeeding at the Workplace produced by the NTUC, U Family, Health Promotion Board, and SNEF. Additionally, the Building and Construction Authority's Code on Accessibility legally requires building owners to provide lactation facilities. The Ministry emphasizes working with tripartite partners to foster an inclusive work culture rather than imposing strict regulatory constraints on employers.

results.title: Key Lessons and Improvements following Recent LRT and MRT Disruptions
results.summary: Questions regarding LTA's enforcement of delay notification protocols, associated operator penalties, and the potential revision of the MRT Mean Kilometres Between Failures (MKBF) target to exceed 1 million have been formally addressed. The Acting Minister for Transport directed that these inquiries be answered via Parliamentary Questions Nos 19 to 24 at the 22 September 2025 Sitting. Specific details on penalty amounts, historical non-adherence instances, comparative international MKBF data, and the rationale for increasing reliability targets were not provided in this transcript.

results.title: Levels of Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS) in Singapore’s Drinking Water from 2016 to 2025
results.summary: Since 2016, the Public Utilities Board (PUB) has monitored per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) in Singapore's drinking water via a comprehensive sampling programme. Leveraging advanced laboratory testing, PUB has not detected PFAS in the national water supply. This ongoing surveillance confirms that current drinking water levels remain below detection limits, indicating no immediate regulatory intervention or public health concern regarding PFAS contamination. The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment confirmed these findings during the parliamentary session.

results.title: Long-term Commercial or Industrial Property Vacancies and Impacts on Rental Markets
results.summary: As of June 2025, the total stock of commercial and industrial spaces vacant for over a year stands at 220,000 square metres for retail, 440,000 square metres for office, and 2.7 million square metres for industrial sectors. These figures represent 3.4%, 5.4%, and 5.0% of total available space respectively, which the Minister noted are generally comparable to pre-pandemic levels. The Minister confirmed that studies have been undertaken to investigate the prevalence and scale of these vacancies and their impact on rental volumes and prices, though specific details of the studies were not provided in the response.

results.title: Making Government Business Support Materials and Resources Available in Chinese for SMEs and Micro-enterprises
results.summary: Enterprise Singapore already provides SME support materials, including brochures and grant guides, in Singapore's four official languages: English, Malay, Mandarin, and Tamil. These resources are accessible via the Enterprise Singapore website, ensuring inclusivity for Chinese-speaking business owners without requiring further expansion. Additionally, advisory support in these languages is available through the respective Singapore Malay, Chinese, and Indian Chambers of Commerce and Industry. The Ministry has not indicated plans to alter this existing multilingual framework.

results.title: Management of Crow-related Public Nuisance and Safety Threats in Residential Areas
results.summary: Since 2024, NParks has removed over 8,600 crow nests and 16,000 crows from urban hotspots. In response to increased public feedback, the Ministry of National Development and NParks are collaborating with the Singapore Police Force to resume controlled shooting for population control. Current management strategies include enhanced trapping, nest removal, enforcement against illegal feeding, and education to reduce food sources. A specific timeline for reviewing the reinstatement of shooting was not provided in the response.

results.title: Mandating PMAs and PABs to Undergo Periodic Inspections to Ensure Compliance with Safety Standards
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that while Personal Mobility Devices undergo biennial inspections, no internationally recognised fire safety standards currently exist for Personal Mobility Aids, preventing similar periodic inspection mandates. Power-Assisted Bicycles must conform to the EN15194 standard, including anti-tampering measures effective since July 2021, before type-approval and registration. The Government will continue monitoring international standard developments for Personal Mobility Aids. The Land Transport Authority enforces strict actions against illegal modifications of all motorised active mobility devices to ensure ongoing safety compliance.

results.title: Measures to Assist Palestine Authority Towards Statehood and Implementation of Two-State Solution
results.summary: Singapore's Foreign Affairs Minister addressed measures to assist the Palestine Authority and accelerate the Two-State Solution during a Ministerial Statement on 22 September 2025. The Minister outlined Singapore's diplomatic efforts but did not specify concrete actions, sanctions, embargoes, or conditions for recognizing Palestine as a state. Details regarding stronger actions in response to military operations in Gaza, blockades, or international humanitarian law violations were not provided in the transcript. The response referred members to the full Ministerial Statements for comprehensive information on Singapore's position and potential future steps.

results.title: Measures to Support PWM Workers Residing in Public Rental Housing Experiencing Rent Increases in View of Wage Adjustments
results.summary: The Housing and Development Board (HDB) does not track Progressive Wage Model (PWM) workers by employment sector. Consequently, HDB will not raise rents for public rental tenants, including PWM workers, during the tenancy term despite income increases. Upon tenancy renewal, rent adjustments will be applied only after considering household size and living expenses to ensure the increase remains lower than the tenant's income rise. This approach ensures tenants remain financially better off when earning higher incomes, as heavily subsidised rental rates are designed to be fair to all taxpayers.

results.title: Mental Health Support for Adults Facing Multiple Stressors and Improved Data for Targeted Intervention
results.summary: The Ministry of Health offers first-stop touchpoints including Mindline 1771, mindline.sg, and Community Outreach Teams for mental distress support. For working adults, tripartite partners issued the Tripartite Advisory on Mental Health and Well-Being at Workplaces, while the Health Promotion Board's Workplace Outreach Wellness Programme provides employers with resources and funding to improve employee well-being. Regarding data, the Immigration and Checkpoints Authority publishes annual suicide death statistics based on the Registry of Deaths. The Minister stated there is currently no comprehensive data source for suicide attempts, limiting the inclusion of indicators like socioeconomic status or employment in published reports.

results.title: Mitigating Adverse Impact to Flora and Fauna in Woodlands Forest in Light of Clearance Commencement in 2026
results.summary: To mitigate adverse impacts on flora and fauna during the 2026 Woodlands forest clearance, the JTC Corporation commissioned an Environmental Impact Assessment reviewed with nature groups and published online in August 2025. The resulting report recommends specific measures including transplanting conservation-significant plant species, implementing a Wildlife Management Plan, and integrating feasible greenery into the Woodlands Gateway development. These actions address concerns regarding critically and endangered species while accommodating necessary land use for new homes and jobs within Singapore's tight constraints. Related issues raised by Ms Poh Li San will be addressed in a subsequent Sitting.

results.title: Monitoring Humanitarian Relief Efforts by Private Organisations and Aid Delivery Restrictions to Gaza
results.summary: The Minister for Foreign Affairs directed that the written response regarding government monitoring of private humanitarian initiatives like Rahmatan Lil Alamin Foundation and Humanity Matters, as well as restrictions on aid delivery to Gaza, was provided in ministerial statements on 22 September 2025. The transcript does not contain the specific details of those statements, the operational constraints on aid contributions collected from Singaporeans, or the recommended methods for citizens to contribute to Middle East relief efforts. Consequently, the specific policy signals, eligibility conditions, and actionable guidance requested in the question remain unstated in this record.

results.title: Number of and Criteria for Private Property Owners Given Waivers for Mandatory Waiting Period
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the number of private property owners granted waivers for the 15-month HDB resale waiting period is already provided in the response to Parliamentary Question No 112, scheduled for oral answer on the Order Paper on 22 September 2025. The Minister did not address whether changes in family circumstances, such as space or financial adequacy, are considered factors when granting these waivers. Consequently, the specific criteria regarding family circumstances remain unstated in this transcript. Users seeking the exact waiver count must refer to the cited parliamentary response for the complete data.

results.title: Percentage of Pre-2018 HDB Units Lacking Home Fire Alarm Devices and Cost Benefit Analysis Mandating Compulsory Installation of Such Devices
results.summary: As of June 2025, Home Fire Alarm Devices (HFADs) are installed in 17% of all HDB flats, with 39% of pre-2018 units covered. While mandatory for new homes since 2018, existing units face a risk-calibrated approach requiring installation only during fire safety alterations, though over 80% of public rental flats already have them. The Ministry encourages voluntary installation via subsidies up to 95% under the Enhanced Active Seniors programme and free installation for public rental tenants. HDB will progressively cover the remaining 20% of public rental flats. The Minister stated there is currently no need to mandate HFADs in HDB corridors or implement centralised building fire management systems, though fire safety reviews will continue.

results.title: Management of Bullying in Schools
results.summary: The Ministry of Education maintains a firm stance against all hurtful behaviours, employing a tiered disciplinary framework that ranges from reflective learning for minor incidents to suspension, detention, or caning for egregious cases involving boys. Investigations are conducted by schools before actions are decided, with educators granted autonomy to adjust measures based on context while prioritising the rehabilitation of victims and perpetrators. Regarding the Sengkang Green Primary case, the Ministry emphasises that existing guidelines and reporting channels are sufficient, though specific timelines for that investigation were not detailed in the notes. Toolkits and best practices are available online and in hard copy for parents, while training on early identification is provided to leaders, educators, counsellors, and welfare officers. The Ministry is also evaluating preventative measures and integrating upstanding behaviour into the core CCE curriculum, though the status of a centralised anti-bullying unit or specific penalties for staff remains unstated.

results.title: Potential COE Premium Spike in Late 2025 Due To Higher Vehicle Demand Before Revision of Electric Vehicle Rebates
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that COE prices remain a function of supply and demand, noting that supply will continue increasing until the peak in 2026. To mitigate potential premium spikes ahead of revised electric vehicle rebates, the government has injected up to 20,000 additional COE quotas since February 2025 and brought forward future guaranteed de-registration quotas. While specific measures to ensure market stability and affordable ownership were not detailed beyond these supply-side actions, the Minister encouraged buyers to be prudent given the anticipated quota availability in coming quarters. Further context was directed to the response given to the Adjournment Motion on 22 September 2025.

results.title: Progress of Take-up Rate of NEA's Incubation Stall Programme
results.summary: As of 30 June 2025, the National Environment Agency has allocated 99 Incubation Stalls to aspiring hawkers, with approximately 37 having converted to full-fledged operations. The programme offers pre-fitted stalls with reduced rental rates, including a 50% discount for 15 months, following a 2019 extension based on participant feedback. NEA conducts periodic reviews to ensure scheme relevance and will continue monitoring the initiative to support business viability. The Minister noted that achieving sustainability depends on hawkers' ability to meet patron expectations and manage operations effectively.

results.title: Proposal for New Aggravated Cruelty Category in Legislation for Cases Resulting in Death or Permanent Disability of Animals
results.summary: The Minister for National Development directed that the query regarding a review of the Animals and Birds Act to introduce an aggravated cruelty category for cases resulting in death or permanent disability, along with proposed definitions and the rationale for the current 18-month maximum jail term, has been addressed via a written reply. This response was issued for Question Nos 64, 65, 66, and 67 on the Order Paper dated 22 September 2025. No further oral details or policy signals were provided during this parliamentary session.

results.title: Proposed Task Force on Electrical Consumer Goods Fire Safety
results.summary: The Minister for Home Affairs stated that HDB fires have declined from 939 in 2020 to 803 in 2024. The Singapore Civil Defence Force already collaborates systematically with agencies including the Land Transport Authority, HDB, and People's Association to minimise fire risks. The Government will continue leveraging these existing partnerships rather than establishing a new dedicated Task Force on Electrical Consumer Goods Fire Safety. No specific new measures or timelines for a separate task force were announced.

results.title: Prosecution and Police Investigations into Offences under Part 9 of Administration of Muslim Law Act 1966
results.summary: The Acting Minister-in-charge of Muslim Affairs clarified that suspected offences under Part 9 of the Administration of Muslim Law Act 1966 (AMLA) are identified via public complaints, mosque engagements, or other channels, not exclusively through MUIS reports. While MUIS may pursue non-prosecutorial measures like public education, counselling, or issuing fatwas depending on case severity, it can lodge police reports where necessary. Police investigations and charging decisions by the Attorney-General's Chambers remain independent of MUIS actions. The Minister noted that court prosecution is not always appropriate for private life matters, citing one recent conviction for teaching false doctrine under Section 139 of AMLA.

results.title: Providing English-language Programmes Equivalent to Autonomous Universities' Mandarin Postgraduate Programmes
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed that English remains the primary medium of instruction at autonomous universities, where Mandarin-medium postgraduate programmes constitute less than 5% of total offerings. Most existing Mandarin programmes have English-taught equivalents or are delivered bilingually. These specific programmes were developed to meet demand from students targeting Mandarin-speaking markets and are open to all applicants. The Ministry noted that autonomous universities retain full autonomy in designing their postgraduate curriculum portfolios.

results.title: Public Education Campaign to Address Fire Incidents in HDB Flats
results.summary: Fire incidents in HDB flats have declined from 939 in 2020 to 803 in 2024. The Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF) conducts regular outreach, including free hands-on training via the Responders Plus Programme on fire extinguishers, CPR, and AEDs. These skills are promoted under the SGSecure national movement. Additionally, SCDF and People Association Community Emergency and Engagement Committees regularly conduct block evacuation exercises to ensure residents are equipped to handle emergencies. No comprehensive nation-wide campaign was explicitly proposed, as existing measures are deemed sufficient.
